articles.title: Roasts, Jokes, and Awkward Moments: The White House Dinner That Everyone Talks About
articles.summary: Have you ever seen a president get roasted by a comedian? At the White House Correspondents' Dinner, it happens every year! This year, Donald Trump will attend for the first time as president. The dinner has a long history of funny and awkward moments. In 1983, Ronald Reagan decided not to tell jokes because U.S. soldiers had just been killed in Lebanon. In 2004, George W. Bush made fun of himself for not finding weapons in Iraq. And in 2006, comedian Stephen Colbert shocked everyone by making fun of President Bush right to his face. The dinner is where the president, journalists, and celebrities all hang out—and get teased!

articles.title: A Carousel With a Story: How a Merry-Go-Round Helped Change History
articles.summary: A very special carousel just reopened in Washington, D.C., after three years of repairs. But this isn't just any merry-go-round. It has a powerful history. In the 1960s, this carousel was at an amusement park near Baltimore that was only for white people. Black children and their families weren't allowed to ride. After years of protests by Black and white activists, the park finally let everyone in on August 28, 1963—the same day Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. gave his "I Have a Dream" speech. The first Black child to ride was 11-month-old Sharon Langley. This week, 63-year-old Sharon rode again on a horse named "Freedom Rider." The carousel is now a symbol of the fight for equality.

articles.title: Tennis Star Carlos Alcaraz Will Miss the French Open Because of a Wrist Injury
articles.summary: Bad news for tennis fans! Carlos Alcaraz, the two-time French Open champion, won't play in this year's tournament. The 22-year-old star hurt his right wrist during a match in Barcelona. At first, he hoped it wasn't serious, but tests showed he needs to rest. He'll also miss the Italian Open. Alcaraz became the youngest man ever to win all four major tennis tournaments (the career Grand Slam) when he won the Australian Open in January. He's ranked number 2 in the world. His rival, Jannik Sinner, said, "It's sad news. You want to play against the best." Sinner hopes Alcaraz will be back for Wimbledon in July. Alcaraz said he'd rather miss tournaments now than risk making the injury worse.
